item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Replying here for greater visibility as this post is at the top of the page.

The image sourced is from a still from this music video, and is therefore not compliant with the SCP Wiki's CC-BY-SA 3.0 license. As such, the image has been removed. Make sure to follow our image use policy when picking any future images to make sure the license is compatible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What concrete evidence does the foundation have that these are not an elaborate hoax? Its implied that some of the videos are of questionable quality, which would make faking the content all the bit more easy.

Yes you said that its anomalous, but you haven't provided enough evidence to support that statement.
You provide weak evidence of it being anomalous in that researchers and subjects all agree that it sounds really good. Is that enough to say its anomalous? Does that mean that Elvis or the Beatles should be contained?

The only definitive evidence I can see is that the accounts are untraceable but that doesn't prove that the videos are real, there is equally a possibility that the uploads are anomalous and even accepting the prior fact uncritically, that the content of the videos is not real.

As far as I can see the only other evidence of it being anomalous is that it has a cliche story associated with it about humans finding and reverse engendering alien technology, but how exactly does the foundation know that its not fake? If if they don't know that it is real, then why are they assuming that its real so easily?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exsept keeping people in jail based off a pycological prolife instead of trying to work with that prolife (which I asume is the case or else there wouldn't be noticiable amounts of outcry over it)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I wish this was more popular. It's such a fascinating and creative SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never seen a story concept anything like this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a little less fun with the new image, but I guess that might be the cost of doing business. I'd be up for trying to 'shop something, if you had any thoughts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Last line of the interview is accredited to the wrong person, but otherwise I like it. On the whole, though, this doesn't really catch me, though at least you did something original with an alternate timeline skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: crits duly noted and interview spruced up. thanks y'all

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Title: [REDACTED]
Runtime: 02:23
Description: News broadcast detailing the construction of what appears to be a large, black speaker-like structure, approximately 1 KM wide located an uninhabited expanse of desert. The broadcast is in an unknown language.

Please let this be the building of a stage for Disaster Area.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Admittedly, there's going to be no way to avoid that if you use an image off of the internet wholesale. I could recognize it the moment I opened the article, and it did dim the immersion a little.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Uploaded videos contain footage of musical performances, interviews, and advertisements featuring unknown musical artists and related figures.

The picture used is from the K-pop band Stellar, specifically the song Study. It's hard to take the article seriously from an immersion perspective since the artistes can be identified by anyone familiar with the stylistics of K-pop.1

EDIT: Since the picture is changed, the point above is effectively moot (unless someone can identify the current image to be from a pre-existing MV/artiste).

Footnotes
1. I'm not even a fan of K-pop, although I can recognise the aesthetics and themes common to the genre.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted heavily because it tripped my Macross fanboy sensors in a great way, in addition to being a solid article. (Super sparse and somewhat cliche in the description but kicking in the addenda. I might've preferred the balance line a bit off from that, but it worked well.)

(Edit: Also, not calling this a Macross ripoff. It just shares major thematic elements.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Despite the obvious popularity of these artists,

That part seems odd to me: "obvious popularity" implies that they're popular in real life, but the article states that they don't correspond to actual artists. "Despite the depicted popularity" sounds more fitting; I'm sure there's also a better phrasing somewhere out there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, it starts of nice and whimsy, does get a bit darker towards the end.I have no problem upvoting this. However

which led subjects to conclude the concert is taking place in a dirigible-like craft.

I swear, our entire timeline is an anomaly for the fact that it's the only one that doesn't use zeppelins for everything.
